title: Boat Rental in Barbados: How to Choose the Right Vessel in 2026
html:
When planning a boat rental in Barbados, start from Bridgetown or the west coast to explore calm Caribbean waters. Prices for a half-day motorboat typically range from EUR 250 to EUR 600, while crewed catamarans cost about EUR 150 per person. BoatHire24 makes it easy to compare verified boat listings directly from owners.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Barbados?

Renting a boat in Barbados costs between EUR 100 per person for a shared tour and EUR 5,000 per day for a crewed luxury yacht. The price depends on boat type, duration, and crew requirements. Shared catamaran trips offer the lowest per-person cost, while private motor yachts command a premium for exclusivity and space.

For a typical half-day rental of a 6-metre motorboat, expect to pay around EUR 300. Full-day catamaran charters for up to twelve guests often cost EUR 1,200 to EUR 2,000. Crewed yachts, such as the Maiora 26m, can exceed EUR 4,000 per day. You can compare verified boat listings on BoatHire24 to find options that match your budget.

Where to set off in Barbados

Most boat trips depart from marinas and harbours on the sheltered west and south coasts. Bridgetown’s Deep Water Harbour is the main commercial port and a common departure point for larger yachts. Port St. Charles in the north offers a full-service marina with easy access to the west coast beaches. The south coast has Oistins Bay, known for its fish market and calm waters. Smaller anchorages include Holetown and Speightstown on the west coast, which provide convenient launch points for day trips. Carlisle Bay, just south of Bridgetown, is popular for snorkelling and swimming with turtles.

Which boat to rent in Barbados

  1. Motorboat (open console): Ideal for a short half-day trip along the west coast, carrying up to 6 passengers from EUR 250.
  2. Catamaran: Best for full-day group excursions with snorkelling stops, accommodating up to 12 guests from EUR 1,500.
  3. Sailboat (monohull): Suited to those who enjoy a more traditional sailing experience, often with a skipper, for up to 8 guests from EUR 800.
  4. Crewed motor yacht: Designed for luxury day trips or week-long charters, sleeping up to 10 guests aboard this luxury yacht starting from EUR 4,500 per day.
  5. Personal watercraft (RIB): Fast and nimble for exploring small coves, carrying up to 4 guests from EUR 200 per hour.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Barbados?

No, you do not need a boating licence to rent a bareboat motorboat in Barbados if the boat is under a certain power threshold, but the operator confirms exact requirements. Most rental companies provide a skipper for larger vessels, which removes any licence concerns. For a self-drive experience, operators may require a short orientation and proof of basic handling skills.

Local tip: The windiest months are January to June, when the north-east trade winds consistently blow at 15-20 knots, making the west coast the calmest and most reliable for novice boaters.

If you plan to sail, consider that Barbados lies outside the hurricane belt, but the high season for charter runs from December to April. During this time, NE trade winds prevail and seas remain moderate. Always confirm the exact licence policy with your charter company, as some may request a valid ICC or equivalent.

At a glance

  • Price range: EUR 100 per person to EUR 5,000 per day
  • Peak months: December to April
  • Typical trip length: half-day (4 hours) or full-day (6-8 hours)
  • Maximum capacity: up to 12 guests on most catamarans
  • Currency: EUR (quoted by BoatHire24 for European travellers)

Questions about boat rental in Barbados

Can I rent a boat in Barbados without a skipper?

Yes, you can rent a small motorboat without a skipper if you have boating experience and the operator deems you competent.

What is the best time of year to rent a boat in Barbados?

The best time is between December and April, when the weather is driest and seas are calm, although the trade winds can be strong.

How long does a typical boat rental last in Barbados?

Most rentals are for half-day (four hours) or full-day (six to eight hours), with some operators offering sunset cruises of two hours.

Are there any hidden costs when renting a boat in Barbados?

Additional costs may include fuel surcharges, crew gratuities (typically 10-15%), and a damage deposit of around EUR 500.

After you have chosen the ideal vessel, consider listing your own boat on BoatHire24 to reach thousands of travellers. You can list a boat for free and manage bookings online. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Barbados or list your own.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Barbados and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
